decks.notes: The deck focuses on deploying disruptive creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Recruiter of the Guard to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also utilizing Aether Vial to cheat creatures into play. The deck also includes efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Thoughtseize to control the board. The goal is to apply pressure quickly with aggressive creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Solitude, while also using cards like Stoneforge Mystic to find powerful equipment to further enhance the board presence. The mana base is designed to consistently support the deck's strategy and disrupt the opponent's mana base with Wasteland.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on creating a strong board presence early in the game by efficiently playing creatures and using cards that benefit from attacking. The deck includes powerful aggressive creatures like Sand Scout and Amped Raptor, supported by Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Hexing Squelcher and Static Prison to hinder the opponent's strategies. With a mix of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and utility lands like Karakas, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures and overwhelming the opponent with a combination of efficient beaters and disruptive spells. The deck prioritizes early game aggression with creatures like Amped Raptor and Sand Scout, supported by removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides a powerful finisher, while Hexing Squelcher and Static Prison disrupt the opponent's game plan. The land base includes utility lands like Wasteland and Karakas to further disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to win quickly through a combination of creature damage and disruption.

This Eldrazi Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying powerful Eldrazi creatures to overwhelm opponents. By using mana ramp cards like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb, the deck aims to establish a board presence early and disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Chalice of the Void. The deck also includes cards like Reality Smasher and Thought-Knot Seer to apply pressure and disrupt the opponent's hand. With a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells, this deck aims to close out games quickly and efficiently.

The deck revolves around using the Aluren combo to cheat in powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Acererak the Archlich. It also includes cards like Show and Tell and Omniscience to facilitate this strategy. Formidable Speaker and Atraxa, Grand Unifier provide support for the combo pieces. The deck includes control elements like Force of Will and counterspells, along with card selection with Brainstorm and Ponder. The mana base includes a mix of dual lands, fetchlands, and utility lands like Hedge Maze and Boseiju, Who Endures.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Aluren combo, which allows you to cast creatures without paying their mana cost. By using cards like Formidable Speaker, Acererak the Archlich, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, you can generate powerful combos and overwhelm your opponent. The deck also includes strong control elements like Force of Will and Brainstorm to protect your combo pieces and disrupt your opponent's strategy. With the support of cards like Show and Tell and Omniscience, you can quickly establish board dominance and secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing lands to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It aims to assemble the Dark Depths/Thespian's Stage combo to create a 20/20 Marit Lage token while using cards like Crop Rotation and Life from the Loam to find key pieces and protect the combo. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Wasteland and Boseiju, Who Endures to hinder the opponent's plays. Overall, the deck relies on resource management and strategic land plays to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing both control and tempo elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gaining incremental advantage through card advantage and powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Stoneforge Mystic. By using a mix of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw, the deck aims to control the board and protect its own threats to ultimately secure victory. The inclusion of cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Quantum Riddler adds additional card advantage and synergy to the deck, further enhancing its ability to control the game.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling the Aluren combo to enable powerful plays such as cheating out Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Acererak the Archlich. It includes protection cards like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to ensure the combo goes off smoothly. The deck also includes various draw and search cards like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Stock Up to find key pieces. Additionally, the inclusion of Show and Tell and Omniscience allows for alternative win conditions. The mana base is designed to support casting the combo pieces quickly and efficiently.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying and utilizing artifacts to gain advantage and disrupt opponents. It uses cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Urza's Saga to generate value, while also including disruptive elements like Chalice of the Void and Force of Will. The goal is to overwhelm opponents with artifact creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Patchwork Automaton, while controlling the game with spells like Sink into Stupor. The deck aims to be proactive and aggressive, leveraging its artifact synergies to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck focuses on dealing direct damage to the opponent quickly while also preventing them from gaining life or stabilizing their board. By playing aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Goblin Guide alongside efficient burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Spike, the goal is to reduce the opponent's life total to zero as fast as possible. Price of Progress and Sulfuric Vortex also punish opponents for playing non-basic lands and gaining life, respectively. This deck aims to win the game before the opponent can establish a strong defense or stabilize their life total.

This deck focuses on using creatures with disruptive abilities like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Skyclave Apparition, along with cards like Aether Vial and Stoneforge Mystic to put pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan. The deck also includes powerful equipment like Batterskull and Meteor Sword to increase the threat level of its creatures. Karakas is included to provide protection from legendary threats, while Wasteland and Ghost Quarter disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure and disrupt the opponent's strategy to secure a quick victory.

The focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying threats quickly and efficiently. The deck aims to control the board through counter magic and removal spells, while utilizing efficient creatures like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf to apply pressure and close out the game. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and filtering, while cards like Thoughtseize and Daze help to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to ensure consistent and powerful draws throughout the game.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of utilizing lands and disruptive spells. The main strategy involves quickly assembling the Thespian's Stage/Dark Depths combo to create a powerful 20/20 Marit Lage token. The deck also uses cards like Life from the Loam and Exploration to ensure a steady stream of land drops and to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Wasteland and Boseiju, Who Endures. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to lock down the board and overwhelm the opponent with a quick and powerful win condition.

The deck aims to win by quickly filling the graveyard with combo pieces and using graveyard recursion to bring back key cards such as Thassa's Oracle and Narcomoeba. By casting a series of spells and sacrificing creatures, the player can trigger an infinite loop to win the game. The deck also includes cards for protection and disruption, such as Pact of Negation and Thoughseize, to ensure the combo goes off smoothly. The strategy relies on speed, precision, and careful sequencing to execute the combo successfully and overwhelm the opponent.

This deck focuses on using Painter's Servant to turn all cards in the opponent's deck the same color, then using Grindstone to mill them out in one turn. It includes various ways to protect the combo, such as counterspells and removal, as well as mana acceleration to get the combo pieces out quickly. The deck also includes cards like Goblin Welder and Goblin Engineer to help find and reuse key artifacts. The goal is to assemble the combo as quickly as possible while disrupting the opponent's game plan.
